name: Crocea Mors
description: Crocea Mors, meaning 'Yellow Death' in Latin, is the sword of Julius Caesar in Arthurian legend. It is often depicted as a formidable weapon, though its significance varies depending on the telling of the Arthurian tales. Some accounts attribute the sword to Nennius, a British warrior who fought against Caesar during his invasion of Britain. Nennius is said to have wrestled the sword from Caesar himself, mortally wounding the Roman Emperor in the process. Nennius, however, also suffered a fatal wound from the sword's initial blow, and the blade remained embedded in his shield until his death. Other versions suggest Crocea Mors was simply captured by British forces. The sword is associated with victory and power, serving as a symbol of British resistance against Roman rule. The sword's name, 'Yellow Death,' alludes to the potential lethality of the blade or, perhaps, to a yellowish hue of the metal itself. Though not as widely known as Excalibur, Crocea Mors remains an important artifact within the broader Arthurian cycle, representing the complex interplay between Roman history and British myth. Its presence underscores the conflict between invading forces and the native people of Britain, forever marking the land with the memory of battles fought and victories won, even at great cost. It is a reminder of the strength and resilience required to defend one's homeland against seemingly insurmountable odds.
subtitle: The Yellow Death of Julius Caesar
origin: Seized from Julius Caesar during his invasion of Britain
currentLocation: Unknown, lost to history or legend

keyMyths.title: Nennius's Victory
keyMyths.description: Nennius wrestles Crocea Mors from Caesar, mortally wounding him but also suffering a fatal blow. The sword remains embedded in his shield until his death.

keyMyths.title: Capture of the Sword
keyMyths.description: In some accounts, Crocea Mors is simply captured by British forces after a battle with Caesar's legions. It becomes a symbol of British victory.

sources.author: Nennius
sources.description: An early historical account that mentions Nennius's battle with Caesar and the acquisition of Crocea Mors.
sources.text: An early historical account that mentions Nennius's battle with Caesar and the acquisition of Crocea Mors.

sources.author: Geoffrey of Monmouth
sources.description: A pseudo-historical account that contributes to the Arthurian legend and may reference Crocea Mors.
